perturbation.original_text: 2.04 NON-SOLICITATION AND NON-INTERFERENCE. Seller agrees that for a period of one (1) year after the execution of this Agreement, it will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for itself or on behalf of or in conjunction with any other person, partnership, firm or corporation hire any employee of the Division or Purchasers as of the Closing 3 <PAGE> 4 Date, or request or induce any employee of the Division or Purchasers as of the Closing Date to terminate his or her employment and accept employment with another entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.04 NON-SOLICITATION AND NON-INTERFERENCE. Seller agrees that for a period of one (1) year after the execution of this Agreement, it will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for itself or on behalf of or in conjunction with any other person, partnership, firm or corporation recruit any employee of the Division or Purchasers as of the Closing 3 <PAGE> 4 Date, or request or induce any employee of the Division or Purchasers as of the Closing Date to terminate his or her employment and accept employment with another entity.
perturbation.explanation: The term "hire" is replaced with "recruit." While seemingly similar, "recruit" can imply a more passive activity than "hire." This substitution creates an in-text contradiction, because "hire" is used in the non-compete agreement, 1.02, and its definition would be different, thus creating confusion on its scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.02 "COMPETE" OR "COMPETING" shall mean with respect to the Business: (i) the design, manufacture, marketing, sale, leasing or distribution of health care products of the types or performing the functions of the products designed, manufactured, marketed, sold, leased and distributed by Seller through the Division as of the date of this Agreement; (ii) hiring, soliciting or attempting to hire or solicit any employee of the Division or Purchasers as of the Closing Date either on Seller's behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity; or (iii) entering into or attempting to enter into any business substantially similar to the Business, either alone or with any individual, partnership, corporation or association; provided, however, that Competing shall not mean or include the ownership, disposition or use of Excluded Assets.
perturbation.justification: The change from "hire" to "recruit" in Section 2.04 introduces a potential contradiction with Section 1.02, where "hiring" is explicitly defined as part of "COMPETE" or "COMPETING." The explanation highlights that "recruit" is a more passive activity than "hire." If "recruit" is interpreted more narrowly, it may not be covered under "COMPETE," creating an ambiguity about whether recruiting, but not hiring, an employee violates the non-compete agreement. Section 1.02 explicitly states that attempting to hire or solicit any employee is considered competing. The modified language in 2.04 introduces a degree of separation that makes the sections incompatible, fitting the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2.05 REMEDIES. Seller acknowledges that any violation of this Article II will cause irreparable harm to Purchasers and Parent and that damages are not an adequate remedy. Seller, therefore, agrees that Purchasers and Parent shall be entitled to an injunction, without the necessity of posting any bond, enjoining, prohibiting, and restraining Seller from the continuance of any such violation, in addition to any monetary recovery that might be available to Purchasers and Parent by reason of a violation of this Agreement and any other remedies at law or in equity, including without limitation specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.05 REMEDIES. Seller acknowledges that any breach of this Article II will cause irreparable harm to Purchasers and Parent and that damages are not an adequate remedy. Seller, therefore, agrees that Purchasers and Parent shall be entitled to an injunction, without the necessity of posting any bond, enjoining, prohibiting, and restraining Seller from the continuance of any such violation, in addition to any monetary recovery that might be available to Purchasers and Parent by reason of a violation of this Agreement and any other remedies at law or in equity, including without limitation specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'violation' to 'breach' creates ambiguity. 'Violation' generally suggests breaking a specific rule, while 'breach' is broader, suggesting failure to perform a duty or obligation. This introduces contradiction because the specific acts that constitute a violation or breach are not enumerated, thus creating confusion. In 2.05, breach now encompasses a broad range of failures, but the specific remedy, 'enjoining, prohibiting, and restraining Seller from the continuance of any such violation' still employs 'violation'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.05 REMEDIES. Seller acknowledges that any breach of this Article II will cause irreparable harm to Purchasers and Parent and that damages are not an adequate remedy. Seller, therefore, agrees that Purchasers and Parent shall be entitled to an injunction, without the necessity of posting any bond, enjoining, prohibiting, and restraining Seller from the continuance of any such violation, in addition to any monetary recovery that might be available to Purchasers and Parent by reason of a violation of this Agreement and any other remedies at law or in equity, including without limitation specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'violation' to 'breach' introduces a contradiction because while 'breach' is now used to describe the triggering event, the remedy section still uses the term 'violation' when describing the action the seller is enjoined from doing. This creates a discrepancy as it is unclear whether the injunction applies only to violations or to all breaches, including those that may not be violations, aligning with the definition of misaligned terminology, which leads to an in-text contradiction when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The change causes the contradiction to arise since the 'Contradicted Text' is an exact copy of the 'Changed Text'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.01 SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS. This Agreement may not be assigned by any party hereto, except that Purchasers' and Parent's rights, duties and obligations under this Agreement may be assigned and delegated to any subsidiary of Purchasers or Parent or to the acquiror of Purchasers, Parent or the Business in the event either Purchaser or Parent is merged, acquired, sells substantially all of its interest in the Business, or transfers its interest in the Business to any other entity. From and after any such assignment by Purchasers or Parent, the term "Purchasers" or "Parent," as the case may be, shall mean such assignee, and such assignee may enforce any of the rights of Purchasers or Parent under this Agreement as if it were the original party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.01 SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS. This Agreement may not be transferred by any party hereto, except that Purchasers' and Parent's rights, duties and obligations under this Agreement may be assigned and delegated to any subsidiary of Purchasers or Parent or to the acquiror of Purchasers, Parent or the Business in the event either Purchaser or Parent is merged, acquired, sells substantially all of its interest in the Business, or transfers its interest in the Business to any other entity. From and after any such assignment by Purchasers or Parent, the term "Purchasers" or "Parent," as the case may be, shall mean such assignee, and such assignee may enforce any of the rights of Purchasers or Parent under this Agreement as if it were the original party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'assigned' to 'transferred' at the beginning of 3.01, in the phrase 'This Agreement may not be assigned', creates an in-text contradiction with the rest of 3.01 where 'assigned' is used. While assignment is a legal term with a defined meaning (transferring rights and obligations), transferring has a broader and less precise meaning. It makes it unclear whether all transfers are prohibited, or only those that aren't assignments, and thus creates confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.01 SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS. This Agreement may not be transferred by any party hereto, except that Purchasers' and Parent's rights, duties and obligations under this Agreement may be assigned and delegated to any subsidiary of Purchasers or Parent or to the acquiror of Purchasers, Parent or the Business in the event either Purchaser or Parent is merged, acquired, sells substantially all of its interest in the Business, or transfers its interest in the Business to any other entity. From and after any such assignment by Purchasers or Parent, the term "Purchasers" or "Parent," as the case may be, shall mean such assignee, and such assignee may enforce any of the rights of Purchasers or Parent under this Agreement as if it were the original party hereto.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'assigned' to 'transferred' in the first sentence of Section 3.01 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text allows assignment under certain conditions. The changed text prohibits 'transfer' which is a broader term. However, the rest of the section refers to 'assignment,' creating ambiguity as to whether transfers that are not assignments are also prohibited, which directly contradicts the later uses of 'assignment' in the same section. As defined in the prompt, misaligned terminology creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Seller hereby acknowledges that the consideration specified herein and in the Asset Sale Agreement is good and valuable consideration received by Seller for the covenants and undertakings described in 4 <PAGE> 5 this Agreement, and such covenants and undertakings are ancillary to, and an integral part of the transactions contemplated by the Asset Sale Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller hereby acknowledges that the remuneration specified herein and in the Asset Sale Agreement is good and valuable consideration received by Seller for the covenants and undertakings described in 4 <PAGE> 5 this Agreement, and such covenants and undertakings are ancillary to, and an integral part of the transactions contemplated by the Asset Sale Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'consideration' with 'remuneration'. Here, the subtle change affects the interpretation of the underlying agreement and its intent. 'Consideration' is a legally binding promise exchanged between parties, as opposed to remuneration which implies a fee in return for services. By changing consideration to remuneration, the seller's acknowledgement is now framed as payment for services, which is in text with article 3.02. This changes the relationship of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.02 INTEGRATED AGREEMENT AND CONSIDERATION. This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement between the parties with regard to its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements (other than the Asset Sale Agreement and the agreements entered into in connection therewith) relating to the same subject matter. Seller hereby acknowledges that the remuneration specified herein and in the Asset Sale Agreement is good and valuable consideration received by Seller for the covenants and undertakings described in 4 <PAGE> 5 this Agreement, and such covenants and undertakings are ancillary to, and an integral part of the transactions contemplated by the Asset Sale Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'consideration' to 'remuneration' alters the fundamental nature of the agreement, framing it as a payment for services rather than a legally binding exchange of promises. This contradicts the understanding and intent of the Asset Sale Agreement described in section 3.02, creating a conflict between the clauses, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.
